title: cerbera salutaris
intro: Cerbera salutaris, also known as the Australian Beach Cherry or Beach Staple, is a plant species in the family Apocynaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Cerbera salutaris are attributed to its ability to lower cholesterol levels, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit anti-cancer properties. Cerbera salutaris is often used in herbal preparations, including decoctions and infusions, which are consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
